jokes.text: Hot sauce? / Kearney, that could blind someone. You get an 'A.'
jokes.setup: Teacher asks for alternative liquids for water balloons, expecting harmless answers
jokes.punchline: Rewarding violent suggestion with top grade
jokes.explanation: Sharp subversion of educational expectations - rewarding dangerous behavior with academic success

jokes.text: Not otto-- he'd drive us off a cliff. / Off a cliff? That would solve everything!
jokes.setup: Teacher avoiding targeting the bus driver for safety reasons
jokes.punchline: Otto enthusiastically endorsing his own dangerous driving as problem-solving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto character moment - his nihilistic enthusiasm for destruction matches his established personality

jokes.text: You've betrayed me for the last time.
jokes.setup: Child's friendship drama on school bus
jokes.punchline: Melodramatic villain dialogue applied to elementary school conflict
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart over-dramatization of minor social conflicts

jokes.text: horseplay on a school bus? Has the world gone mad?
jokes.setup: Bus driver witnessing typical kid behavior
jokes.punchline: Otto treating normal childhood roughhousing as apocalyptic
jokes.explanation: Otto's drug-addled perspective making ordinary events seem cosmic

jokes.text: It's your fault, for giving birth to my archenemy! / At least i was planned.
jokes.setup: Bart blaming Marge for Lisa's existence as his enemy
jokes.punchline: Lisa's devastating comeback about Bart being unplanned
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sibling rivalry escalation - Lisa's intellectual superiority deployed as emotional warfare

jokes.text: stop it! No one was planned!
jokes.setup: Trying to stop kids' fight about planning
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing neither child was wanted
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Marge's attempt to mediate backfires spectacularly with devastating honesty

jokes.text: They have a zipper from every james bond!
jokes.setup: Trying to make Dayton sound exciting
jokes.punchline: Hilariously specific and pointless Bond memorabilia
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd tourism pitch - the specificity makes it funnier

jokes.text: Why won't I die?
jokes.setup: Description of Uncle Tyrone being bitter and depressed
jokes.punchline: His existential despair delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny elderly character moment - brutal honesty about mortality

jokes.text: If you make me get on that plane, I'll give myself diarrhea! I know how!
jokes.setup: Bart desperately trying to avoid the trip
jokes.punchline: Claiming ability to self-induce illness with confidence
jokes.explanation: Pure Bart - disgusting but delivered with such conviction it becomes funny

jokes.text: I'm only rotting on the right side. / I'll right-side you!
jokes.setup: Grampa describing his physical decay
jokes.punchline: Homer turning 'right side' into a threat/action
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that works because of Homer's aggressive stupidity

jokes.text: brideshead's gonna get revisited tonight, baby!
jokes.setup: Homer excited about renting a movie
jokes.punchline: Treating highbrow British literature adaptation like porn
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - sexualizing something completely unsexy with typical enthusiasm

jokes.text: I say you don't exist!
jokes.setup: Clerk saying chocolate star wars doesn't exist
jokes.punchline: Homer escalating to existential denial of the clerk
jokes.explanation: Homer's illogical escalation pattern - denying reality when frustrated

jokes.text: It's about a harvard chick that puts out for a guy... and then croaks. And it saved paramount studios for Charlie Bluhdorn.
jokes.setup: Describing Love Story as a tearjerker
jokes.punchline: Reducing it to crude plot summary plus industry insider knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy - combining vulgarity with pedantic film industry knowledge

jokes.text: A chick flick?! Oh, say it ain't so, ho!
jokes.setup: Bart horrified by romantic movie choice
jokes.punchline: Rhyming plea that accidentally calls Homer a prostitute
jokes.explanation: Bart's attempt at dramatic expression accidentally becomes insult

jokes.text: Like what? They'll do something with you that they hate? / Exactly.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining benefits of watching romantic movies
jokes.punchline: Bart correctly identifying sexual coercion, Homer confirming
jokes.explanation: Dark relationship humor - child seeing through adult manipulation

jokes.text: I say bury her, before she starts to smell.
jokes.setup: Love Story's famous opening about describing a dead woman
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical, crude response to philosophical question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer subversion of romantic sentiment with morbid practicality

jokes.text: She's wooden and unpleasant, and no matter what he does, he's still ryan o'neal.
jokes.setup: Lisa critiquing the romantic movie
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated film criticism ending with personal attack on actor
jokes.explanation: Lisa's precocious film criticism with unexpected personal venom

jokes.text: Yeah, we're tryin' to create a mood here, so shut the hell up!
jokes.setup: Trying to create romantic atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Destroying mood while claiming to protect it
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-defeating approach to romance

jokes.text: Well, I wouldn't be happy.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if Homer would be sad if she died
jokes.punchline: Minimal, unemotional response to hypothetical death
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan - Homer's emotional limitations exposed through understatement

jokes.text: It's called wicca, and it's empowering! / Wicca's a hollywood fad! / That's kabala, jerk!
jokes.setup: Lisa claiming witchcraft powers
jokes.punchline: Bart confusing trendy spiritualities, Lisa correcting
jokes.explanation: Topical humor about celebrity spiritual trends

jokes.text: You know I'm usually good for a triple-x throwdown
jokes.setup: Homer trying to maintain romantic mood
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual boasting inappropriate for family setting
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate oversharing about sex life

jokes.text: You're never alone in this crapshack!
jokes.setup: Marge saying they're alone now
jokes.punchline: Homer's harsh but accurate description of their living situation
jokes.explanation: Homer's brutal honesty about their home life

jokes.text: what kind of an airline routes all their flights through Nome, Alaska? / It's their hub.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning absurd flight routing
jokes.punchline: Marge matter-of-factly accepting airline illogic
jokes.explanation: Absurd airline logistics treated as normal by Marge

jokes.text: not me, I do crunches.
jokes.setup: Seeing young couple, talking about their future
jokes.punchline: Homer's delusional self-assessment of fitness
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical self-delusion about his physical condition

jokes.text: Attention miami passengers, suntan airlines is now boarding rows 'piña colada' through 'daiquiri.'
jokes.setup: Normal airport boarding announcement format
jokes.punchline: Rows named after tropical drinks instead of numbers
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of theme airline marketing

jokes.text: As the bible says: 'Screw that!'
jokes.setup: Homer invoking biblical authority
jokes.punchline: Attributing profane dismissal to scripture
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual blasphemy disguised as religious wisdom

jokes.text: Go ahead, what do I care? I'm getting laid off tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Couple making insane request to change flights
jokes.punchline: Agent's nihilistic acceptance due to impending unemployment
jokes.explanation: Perfect one-off character moment - economic anxiety enabling chaos

jokes.text: you guys are such party animals, we're gonna skip the safety check and just take off!
jokes.setup: Pilot commenting on passengers' spontaneous behavior
jokes.punchline: Applying party attitude to aviation safety
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of reckless abandon into life-threatening territory

jokes.text: A steak, or two steaks? / Can I have both? / Of course.
jokes.setup: First class dining options
jokes.punchline: Homer's greedy interpretation of choice, attendant enabling
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer gluttony moment

jokes.text: I feel like I'm princess grace and princess di smashed together.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing luxury feeling
jokes.punchline: Accidentally invoking two princesses who died tragically
jokes.explanation: Marge's innocent metaphor becomes unintentionally dark

jokes.text: Did you know indonesia is at a crossroads? / No! / It is!
jokes.setup: Homer reading sophisticated publication
jokes.punchline: Treating mundane geopolitical fact as shocking revelation
jokes.explanation: Homer's excitement about boring current events

jokes.text: they might not be dead. They could be in the basement.
jokes.setup: Tornado hitting parents' supposed location
jokes.punchline: Lisa's overly optimistic assessment of survival odds
jokes.explanation: Lisa trying to maintain hope with weak logic

jokes.text: how can I be a parent? I break my teeth on ribbon candy.
jokes.setup: Grampa contemplating guardianship
jokes.punchline: Specific example of his physical incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa moment - specific elderly vulnerability detail

jokes.text: It's really boring. You kids would have hated it.
jokes.setup: Marge lying about being in Dayton
jokes.punchline: Accidentally tells truth about boring trip while lying about location
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Marge accidentally tells truth while lying, revealing her guilt about having fun.

jokes.text: hey, do I hear a back rub? / No, no, that's the noise the phone makes when you've been on too long.
jokes.setup: Lisa detecting suspicious sounds during call
jokes.punchline: Marge's weak excuse for romantic sounds
jokes.explanation: Solid setup/payoff with parent caught in lie

jokes.text: Swelldorado hotel miami beach, 'sun and water without your son and daughter.'
jokes.setup: Hotel phone greeting
jokes.punchline: Slogan explicitly promoting child abandonment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant rhyming slogan that perfectly captures the episode's theme

jokes.text: I can't go to miami. I'm expecting calls from telemarketers!
jokes.setup: Grampa declining trip
jokes.punchline: Treating spam calls as social engagement
jokes.explanation: Perfect elderly loneliness joke - valuing any human contact

jokes.text: All right, ladies, get ready to settle.
jokes.setup: Grampa hearing about available widows
jokes.punchline: Treating romance as desperation exercise
jokes.explanation: Grampa's brutally honest approach to elderly dating

jokes.text: And tonight, we'll finally, and romantically, hump.
jokes.setup: Homer planning romantic evening
jokes.punchline: Crudely destroying romantic mood with word choice
jokes.explanation: Homer's inability to maintain romantic tone

jokes.text: can't wait.
jokes.setup: Young couple excited about having kids
jokes.punchline: Experienced parents' knowing reaction to naive optimism
jokes.explanation: Good parental experience comedy - the knowing look between parents at naive couple.

jokes.text: with the new breast pumps and diaper genies and corporate flex-time, child-rearing ought to be a snap.
jokes.setup: Young couple discussing having children
jokes.punchline: Naive faith in parenting gadgets and workplace flexibility
jokes.explanation: Satirizes naive pre-parent optimism about parenting technology

jokes.text: you keep thinking that.
jokes.setup: Young woman's naive parenting expectations
jokes.punchline: Marge's weary, knowing response
jokes.explanation: Perfect parental wisdom moment - experience vs. naivety

jokes.text: Everything happens to eemore.
jokes.setup: Car problems since Orlando
jokes.punchline: Homer's mispronunciation creates accidental Eeyore reference
jokes.explanation: Homer's mispronunciation accidentally creates Winnie the Pooh reference

jokes.text: No scar. He must be on his first heart. / I wouldn't kick him out of bed for dying.
jokes.setup: Elderly women evaluating Grampa
jokes.punchline: Rating men by medical history and mortality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy about elderly dating priorities and mortality

jokes.text: you know those feet sticking out from under the house in the wizard of oz? You're looking at them.
jokes.setup: Grampa claiming movie background
jokes.punchline: Specific claim about playing dead character in famous scene
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa tall tale - specific, absurd, but oddly believable for him

jokes.text: not the kind of congress that contained paul tsongas...
jokes.setup: Grampa using euphemism for sex
jokes.punchline: Clarifying which meaning of congress, referencing obscure politician
jokes.explanation: Nerdy political reference in context of sexual innuendo

jokes.text: I'm going into the gold medallion club, with silver-level membership.
jokes.setup: Lisa planning to infiltrate airline club
jokes.punchline: Confidently announcing impossible plan
jokes.explanation: Lisa's overconfidence in her rule-bending abilities

jokes.text: and homer's getting the low-fat meal.
jokes.setup: Lisa reading parents' travel details
jokes.punchline: Homer choosing health-conscious option completely out of character
jokes.explanation: Ironic detail showing Homer's dietary inconsistency

jokes.text: You're more boring than my husband, and he's dead.
jokes.setup: Insulting someone as boring
jokes.punchline: Using deceased husband as comparison point
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy setup/punchline - mortality as insult baseline

jokes.text: may I place my hand on your shoulder as a sign of friendship? / Why not?
jokes.setup: Raoul making physical advance disguised as friendship
jokes.punchline: Grampa's oblivious acceptance
jokes.explanation: Setup for uncomfortable situation with Grampa's innocence

jokes.text: Like people in new jersey have never seen a fat man making out. It's on the freakin' state flag.
jokes.setup: Marge worried about people seeing them
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurd claim about New Jersey state flag
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist state pride joke with perfect specificity

jokes.text: I am not as interested in women as my open shirt might suggest. To be frank, they disgust me.
jokes.setup: Raoul's been presenting as womanizer
jokes.punchline: Reveals complete disinterest in women
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation - subverts stereotypical Latin lover archetype with unexpected honesty.

jokes.text: I am not as interested in women as my open shirt might suggest. To be frank, they disgust me.
jokes.setup: Raoul's flirtatious appearance and behavior
jokes.punchline: Direct admission of homosexuality and misogyny
jokes.explanation: Character revelation moment - subverts lothario stereotype

jokes.text: For many years, I was a tater farmer, but the shameful truth is, the taters farmed me.
jokes.setup: Grampa telling story about his past
jokes.punchline: Reversing agricultural relationship in nonsensical way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa rambling nonsense - philosophical absurdity with agricultural twist

jokes.text: Your neck skin dances when you speak.
jokes.setup: Raoul listening to Grampa's story
jokes.punchline: Romantic observation about elderly skin movement
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor - finding beauty in aging body details. Uncomfortable but well-crafted.

jokes.text: This whole family is built on a tissue of lies and romance! / Yeah, it's a tissue, covered in blood and boogers!
jokes.setup: Lisa's sophisticated accusation about family dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Bart's crude elaboration on tissue metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Lisa's eloquence vs Bart's grossness

jokes.text: all I wanted was a second honeymoon, and now the floor is made of lava.
jokes.setup: Homer's romantic dreams being destroyed
jokes.punchline: Contrasting adult desires with children's game
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of adult vs child priorities conflict

jokes.text: Hey, I just zinged you. / I know. My new thing is to ignore you.
jokes.setup: Bart proud of insulting Lisa
jokes.punchline: Lisa's mature dismissal of his juvenile behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic sibling dynamic - Bart seeking attention, Lisa withholding it

jokes.text: Kids don't beat me, I beat kids.
jokes.setup: Homer determined not to be outsmarted
jokes.punchline: Ambiguous phrasing about beating children
jokes.explanation: Standard dark humor - ambiguous phrasing creates uncomfortable double meaning.

jokes.text: Because I was saving that for my birthday.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting car sex as compromise
jokes.punchline: Homer treating it as special occasion activity
jokes.explanation: Homer's weird sexual planning reveals his simple priorities

jokes.text: You've been to space! / And yet, I've never been to me.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out Homer's space experience
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-philosophical response referencing song
jokes.explanation: Homer's accidental profundity referencing Charlene song

jokes.text: back off, canadians. We got them. / You back off, hosers. They're in canadian waters, eh! / Beat it, you puck-slapping maple-suckers! / Take a hike, you shatner-stealing mexico-touchers!
jokes.setup: International jurisdiction dispute
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd national stereotypes as insults
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of national stereotypes, 'Shatner-stealing' particularly clever

jokes.text: I bet it always works up there.
jokes.setup: Homer talking about making love in heaven
jokes.punchline: Assuming sexual performance improves in afterlife
jokes.explanation: Homer's naive assumption about heavenly sexual prowess

jokes.text: More alive than any of you squares!
jokes.setup: Rescue team asks if anyone's alive
jokes.punchline: Homer claims superior aliveness despite near-death
jokes.explanation: Good Homer defiance - claims superiority even in rescue situation. '60s slang adds character.

jokes.text: More alive than any of you squares!
jokes.setup: Rescuers asking if anyone is alive after crash
jokes.punchline: Homer's defiant response despite near-death experience
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate bravado in dangerous situation
